title: junellia bisulcata var. campestris
intro: Junellia bisulcata var. campestris, also known as 'Campestris' or 'Mountain Savin', is a shrub-like plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Junellia bisulcata var. campestris has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential medicinal uses. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, relieve respiratory issues, and treat wounds. Junellia bisulcata var. campestris can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Andean medicine to address various health concerns.
